top_tweets.text:
This is really accurate. 

As much as we all want to drink the vibe coding kool-aid, the reality is that 90%+ of all code in a company is maintaining and migrating existing stuff that is complicated and messy. You can definitely vibe code the remaining 10% but unless there is a
top_tweets.author: chamath
top_tweets.name: Chamath Palihapitiya
top_tweets.summary_zh: 原推指出企業 90% 以上的程式碼都在維護與遷移既有複雜系統，只有剩下 10% 能用 vibe coding 快速開發。此推回應認為這個觀察非常準確，提醒大家不要過度迷信 vibe coding 能解決所有問題。

context.quoted_text:
Every time I see a tweet saying “I can vibe code this in a weekend” - I think of the slack notification system..

It takes time, persistence and effort to get the details right. 

Sure, a lot of simple workflows will get vibe coded away. And maybe you can put this in Claude Code and get the code right in one shot. 

But quality, depth and great systems will still have value and take time. You can’t vibe code lessons. 

Now and forever.

top_tweets.text: As AI agents accelerate coding, what is the future of software engineering? Some trends are clear, such as the Product Management Bottleneck, referring to the idea that we are more constrained by deciding what to build rather than the actual building. But many implications, like
top_tweets.author: AndrewYNg
top_tweets.name: Andrew Ng
top_tweets.summary_zh: AI agents 加速開發後，軟體工程的未來趨勢之一是「產品管理瓶頸」（Product Management Bottleneck）：決定要做什麼的能力，將比實際開發能力更成為限制因素。

top_tweets.text:
5 signs your SaaS design is costing you money:

1. Users need a demo to understand the product
2. Support keeps answering questions your UI should
3. People sign up and never come back
4. Investors ask what does this actually do?
5. You're embarrassed to share the link

If 2+ of https://t.co/aaa3vYU6qx
top_tweets.author: Tanjim38
top_tweets.name: Tanjim | SaaS Product Designer
top_tweets.summary_zh: 五個跡象顯示你的 SaaS 設計正在賠錢：使用者需要 demo 才能理解產品、客服一直在回答 UI 該解釋的問題、註冊後不再回訪、投資人問不懂產品是什麼、你不敢分享連結。

top_tweets.text:
Being a solo-founder:

- 10% coding
- 15% marketing
- 5% operations
- 5% management
- 1% mental control
- 6% UX/UI
- 18% customer support
- 12% sales
- 10% debugging
- 8% product strategy
- 5% finance
- 5% “wtf isn’t this working” existential crisis
top_tweets.author: pmitu
top_tweets.name: Paul Mit
top_tweets.summary_zh: 獨立創辦人的時間分配實況：18% 客服、12% 銷售、10% coding 和 debugging、8% 產品策略、6% UX/UI、5% 運營/管理/財務，以及 5% 的「為什麼這不能動」存在危機。

top_tweets.text:
Vibe coding was a fun trend.

Now it is a massive enterprise risk.

You cannot let 50 people build shadow IT without the guardrails of 
@superblocks
top_tweets.author: KateMillerGems
top_tweets.name: Kate Miller
top_tweets.summary_zh: 原推宣傳 Superblocks 2.0 能讓企業 IT 部門掌控員工 vibe coding 產生的 shadow IT 風險。此推回應指出 vibe coding 已從有趣趨勢變成重大企業風險，不能放任 50 人在沒有守護機制下建立影子系統。

context.quoted_text:
Introducing Superblocks 2.0: AI-generated enterprise apps – finally under IT control.

Vibe-coded apps just became the #1 attack vector in the enterprise.

Business teams are building on production data, while IT has zero visibility.

No reviews. No audits. No permissions. No control.

AI hackers are about to get 100x better. Anthropic proved it with Mythos.

Superblocks 2.0 is the only platform to take back control:

> Business teams build AI-powered apps with permissions baked in.
> IT and Security can audit everything and lock down anything, instantly.
> Engineering sets the standards. Every app follows them.

Instacart, SoFi, and LinkedIn run Superblocks in production today.

And larger organizations we can't yet name are too:

A Fortune 500 just shut down 2,500 Replit users to standardize on Superblocks, running the platform air-gapped in their AWS environment.

A 150,000-employee global services firm replaced Lovable with Superblocks to unlock AI-built apps on restricted internal systems.

Every IT leader we’ve demoed to using Replit, Lovable or v0 asked for early access.

Today we open access to the world.

The genie is out of the bottle on employee vibe coding.

Let it run wild, or take back control – https://t.co/8TEolq14Z5

top_tweets.text:
Farmer doesn't even realize that this is really a right to repair issue. The placement of the bearing is a design choice that takes away the right to repair. This is a conscious decision by a company to make their product unrepairable.

This is not a "quality" issue. It is a
top_tweets.author: longstoryfarms
top_tweets.name: Long Story Farms, LLC
top_tweets.summary_zh: 原推抱怨洗衣機用五年就壞，軸承與滾筒一體成型無法單獨更換。此推指出這不是品質問題，而是設計選擇刻意剝奪維修權（right to repair），是廠商有意識的決策。

context.quoted_text:
The appliance guy told me five years is pretty good on a washer. Five years. 

I bought it in 2019 and the bearing is molded into the drum so you can't just replace the bearing. You replace the whole drum for $900 or you buy a new washer for $985. 

The refrigerator from 1989 sitting in my garage has never been serviced and runs like a top. 

That's your product quality report, folks.
